decks.notes: The main idea of this UR Tempo deck is to aggressively control the board through a combination of counterspells, burn spells, and efficient creatures like Delver of Secrets and Dragon's Rage Channeler. By disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Force of Will and Daze while deploying threats quickly, the deck aims to maintain control of the game tempo and overwhelm the opponent before they can stabilize. Key cards like Brazen Borrower and Murktide Regent provide additional versatility and power to the deck's game plan, making it a formidable force in the Legacy 1 vs 1 format.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game by utilizing lands effectively, particularly by assembling the Dark Depths/Thespian's Stage combo to create a powerful 20/20 creature, while also disrupting the opponent's strategies with cards like Wasteland and Pithing Needle. The deck aims to ramp up quickly with cards like Exploration and Mox Diamond, while also utilizing Life from the Loam to continuously recur lands from the graveyard. Overall, the deck seeks to outmaneuver and outlast opponents through strategic land management and resource denial.

The deck focuses on using a combination of disruptive spells, efficient creatures, and land destruction to maintain tempo, disrupt the opponent's game plan, and apply pressure to win the game quickly. It aims to control the board through countermagic, hand disruption, and removal spells, while also deploying threats like Baleful Strix, Kaito, Murktide Regent, and Tamiyo to deal damage and close out the game. The deck is designed to be aggressive and disruptive, forcing the opponent to react and giving the player a strategic advantage.

This deck aims to quickly assemble a series of spells that allows the player to essentially win the game in one turn by milling their entire deck and either winning with Thassa's Oracle or generating an overwhelming board presence with Bridge from Below. The deck relies on powerful mana acceleration like Lotus Petal, Elvish Spirit Guide, and Simian Spirit Guide to quickly cast spells like Cabal Ritual and Dark Ritual to fuel the combo. Balustrade Spy and Undercity Informer are key pieces to mill the player's entire deck to win the game through their win conditions.

This deck focuses on using artifacts to quickly build up a board presence and overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures. By utilizing cards like Mox Opal, Urza's Saga, and Mishra's Bauble, the deck can generate mana and card advantage while also disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Force of Will and Chalice of the Void. Emry, Lurker of the Loch allows for recursion of artifacts, while Patchwork Automaton and Kappa Cannoneer can apply pressure on the opponent. The deck aims to close out the game quickly with a combination of artifacts and efficient creatures.

The deck aims to quickly and efficiently combo off by casting a high number of spells in a short amount of time, utilizing cards like Dark Ritual, Lotus Petal, and Simian Spirit Guide to generate mana. The deck then leverages cards like Balustrade Spy and Undercity Informer to mill the entire library, triggering win conditions like Thassa's Oracle or Bridge from Below to secure the victory. The deck includes cards like Pact of Negation and Cabal Therapy for protection and disruption, allowing it to quickly go off and win the game before the opponent can react.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize a combination of artifacts and blue spells to create a fast-paced aggressive strategy. With cards like The One Ring, Karn, the Great Creator, and Ugin, Eye of the Storms, the deck aims to control the board and overwhelm the opponent with powerful artifacts. By utilizing cards like Thundertrap Trainer and Stock Up to generate card advantage and resources, along with disruption tools like Pithing Needle and Force of Will, the deck aims to outpace and outsmart the opponent to secure victory quickly.

This Dragon Stompy deck aims to disrupt opponents by locking down their mana base with cards like Blood Moon, Magus of the Moon, and Chalice of the Void while simultaneously deploying powerful threats like Fury and The One Ring to quickly close out the game. By ramping out threats with cards like Chrome Mox and Simian Spirit Guide and utilizing powerful artifacts like Urza's Saga, this deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a fast and aggressive strategy that punishes decks reliant on non-basic lands and non-creature spells.

This deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while applying early pressure with efficient threats like Murktide Regent and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student. It uses disruptive spells like Thoughtseize and Force of Will to control the game tempo. The deck also leverages powerful removal like Fatal Push and Snuff Out to keep the opponent's board clear. With a mix of disruptive elements and aggressive creatures, this deck aims to maintain control of the game while steadily advancing towards victory.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly fill the graveyard with combo pieces such as Narcomoeba and Undercity Informer, then use Dread Return to bring back Balustrade Spy to mill the entire deck and win with Thassa's Oracle. The deck aims to use fast mana accelerants like Lotus Petal and Simian Spirit Guide to cast spells quickly and overwhelm the opponent before they have a chance to respond. It also includes disruptive elements like Cabal Therapy and Thoughtseize to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck is all-in on the combo and seeks to win the game as quickly as possible.

The main objective of this deck is to quickly cycle through the entire deck and use cards like Dark Ritual and Cabal Ritual to generate mana and cast a game-ending combo using cards like Thassa's Oracle and Undercity Informer. By casting spells like Agadeem's Awakening and Pact of Negation, the deck aims to protect its key pieces and go off in a single turn. With the right combination of cards and a bit of luck, this deck can win the game in a spectacular fashion by emptying the entire library and securing victory.

The focus of this deck is to utilize a combination of low-cost creatures and disruptive spells to control the game tempo and apply pressure on the opponent. By using cards like Thoughtseize, Daze, and Force of Will to disrupt the opponent's game plan, while deploying aggressive creatures like Kaito and Nethergoyf to deal damage, the deck aims to maintain control of the board and force the opponent to react defensively. The inclusion of efficient removal spells like Fatal Push and Snuff Out further enhances the deck's ability to control the battlefield.

The deck focuses on using artifacts and powerful planeswalkers to create a lockdown on the opponent, denying them resources and rendering their plays ineffective. The key strategy revolves around using Urza's lands to ramp into big threats like Karn, the Great Creator and Ugin, Eye of the Storms, while utilizing cards like Trinisphere and The One Ring to control the opponent's actions. The goal is to establish a board presence that overwhelms the opponent, allowing for a smooth victory through sheer power and control.

This Dimir Tempo deck focuses on efficient disruption and quick threats to control the board and pressure the opponent. With a mix of powerful creatures like Murktide Regent and Orcish Bowmasters, alongside disruptive spells like Force of Will and Thoughtseize, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while relentlessly attacking. With a balance of card advantage, removal, and tempo plays, this deck aims to outmaneuver the opponent and secure a victory through aggressive gameplay and controlling the pace of the game.

The main focus of this Mardu Aggro deck is to quickly deploy aggressive creatures to apply early pressure on the opponent. The deck utilizes powerful creature cards like Orcish Bowmasters, Amped Raptor, and Ocelot Pride, supported by removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and Thoughtseize to disrupt the opponent's game plan. Additionally, the deck includes cards like Goblin Bombardment for additional reach and Ajani, Nacatl Pariah for board control. The inclusion of disruptive elements like Wasteland and Cabal Therapy further enhances the deck's ability to disrupt the opponent's strategy while maintaining a fast-paced aggressive game plan.

This deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's resources while deploying efficient threats to apply pressure and close out the game quickly. It utilizes a combination of strong removal spells, disruptive elements like Wasteland and Force of Will, and versatile creatures like Uro and Leovold to control the game and outvalue the opponent. The inclusion of cards like Brainstorm and Ponder help ensure consistency and card selection, while also providing ways to dig for answers or threats as needed. The deck aims to establish early dominance and maintain pressure throughout the game.
